title: quercus major
intro: Quercus major, also known as the evergreen oak or Japanese evergreen oak, is a type of evergreen tree native to East Asia. The leaves and bark of the Quercus major plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus major are numerous, including the ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and prevent certain types of cancer, as well as exhibiting anti-aging properties. Quercus major can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
